intro: The biggest practical difference between Orb and Paddle is lock-in. Orb is rated medium lock-in (billing data exportable), while Paddle is rated high lock-in (high — mor model = paddle handles everything, fromwith adapts under paddle). That changes how hard a future migration would be and how much strategy to put into exit planning. Beyond the headline, Orb carries medium lock-in (billing data exportable) and usage-based pricing from $1; Paddle carries high lock-in (high — mor model = paddle handles everything, fromwith adapts under paddle) and usage-based pricing from 5% + $0.50/transaction. Orb is a fit when you are saas. Paddle is a fit when you are saas, b2b. The honest trade-off: Orb is explicitly not for simple projects; Paddle is explicitly not for marketplace/platform payments. On the product side, Orb is described as Developer-first subscription and usage billing platform with a flexible pricing model API and Stripe-compatible webhooks, and Paddle is described as Merchant of Record and subscription billing platform for B2B/B2C SaaS with global tax, checkout, and revenue recovery.
quick_take: Orb is for saas; Paddle is for saas, b2b; decide on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Orb if its medium lock-in is acceptable for your use case and you are saas.
verdict_b: Choose Paddle if you accept higher lock-in in exchange for its specific capabilities and you are saas, b2b.

faq.a: Orb starts at $1; Paddle starts at 5% + $0.50/transaction. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Orb to Paddle?
faq.a: Migration from Orb to Paddle is feasible but not trivial: Orb has medium lock-in (billing data exportable). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

Both tools tie at 5/5 for developer experience on our scoring. The real difference will show up in your specific workflow, not the headline rating. A one-hour spike on both is the fastest way to decide.

faq.q: Is Paddle a good alternative to Orb?
faq.a: Paddle is pitched at saas, b2b, which overlaps with Orb's fit (saas) but is not identical. Note that Paddle is explicitly not for marketplace/platform payments, so it only replaces Orb if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (medium vs high), which is the main thing to weigh.
